channel.title: Posts on Grayson Ricketts
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Grayson Ricketts

item.title: Planning for an objective: Base system
item.description: If there’s something remotely important, it helps to have a plan1. This is the system I use for planning. Assumptions There are scenarios where this system doesn’t work perfectly, but with minor modifications the system can respond to the new scenarios. I’ll go into how to handle those scenarios in another post, but before we augment the system we have to lay out the system. In order to layout the system here are some simplifying assumptions.

item.title: My purpose and values, going into 2022
item.description: We’re purpose and value driven people. As much as our world seems to be logical and thoughtful, our perceptions deceive us. We are ruled by automatic and emotional guidance1. Last year I started to consciously review what my values were2 and revisit them every week. It gave me a grounding point to make sure my emotions and automatic systems weren’t carrying me away from what is important. This year I’m making them public.

item.title: Team Routines
item.description: A team should have a balance of process, efficiency, and autonomy. Here’s a rough outline of the process our team has been using1. The “why’s” and reasonings behind them are further below. Deep retrospectives every three weeks Shallower retrospectives at the start of the week, every week, before we plan Daily check-ins at the start of the day to discuss blockers, coordinate collaboration, and build camaraderie Planning is done weekly and quarterly Most work is picked up by someone rather than assigned out at the start of the week Goals I set up routines to achieve a set of goals.

item.title: Personal Metrics
item.description: Is there something you can do that will make you twice as good at your job? What about more than twice as good? What about just better? There is an innate part of us that strives to improve. This is my story of trying to use metrics to improve. The ultimate goal was to find some combination of behaviors that made a day the best day it could be (or made a bad day bad).

item.title: Experiments
item.description: Every new year resolutions are made yet a month later many have been given up. The idea of a resolution is awesome; make a commitment to achieve or build a new habit that will positively affect your life. But there’s always that fact that I and everyone knows: most resolutioners will quit before February. Rather than resolve to one big thing why not try something else? Try a smaller thing?
